raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should \"Check this\" mean primarily a correctness/claims check, a writing-polish check, or a broad research-draft check covering both?","rationale":{"R1.2":"A generic check of this research passage centrally includes whether the argument from method description to experimental results to conclusions is internally coherent.","R1.3":"The artifact makes empirical claims from reported benchmark numbers, so checking whether those claims match the stated results is a central responsibility.","R1.6":"Because the delegation is simply \"Check this,\" sentence-level clarity, register, and phrasing are plausibly part of the expected check.","RX.1":"Any projection of such a vague request must disclose uncertainty about whether the user intended technical validation, writing polish, or both.","RX.2":"The artifact contains broad superiority and generalization claims, making overclaim avoidance load-bearing for a responsible check.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities and avoid actually revising or evaluating the artifact.","RX.4":"The projection should account for possible downstream harm from endorsing unsupported research claims or exaggerated applicability.","RX.5":"Responsibility should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the specific claims present in the artifact."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should "Check this" mean primarily a correctness/claims check, a writing-polish check, or a broad research-draft check covering both?

rationale.R1.2: A generic check of this research passage centrally includes whether the argument from method description to experimental results to conclusions is internally coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes empirical claims from reported benchmark numbers, so checking whether those claims match the stated results is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation is simply "Check this," sentence-level clarity, register, and phrasing are plausibly part of the expected check.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of such a vague request must disclose uncertainty about whether the user intended technical validation, writing polish, or both.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains broad superiority and generalization claims, making overclaim avoidance load-bearing for a responsible check.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities and avoid actually revising or evaluating the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should account for possible downstream harm from endorsing unsupported research claims or exaggerated applicability.
rationale.RX.5: Responsibility should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the specific claims present in the artifact.
